anchor.snippet: a ball is the solid figure bounded by a sphere
It may be a closed ball (including the boundary points that constitute the sphere) or an open ball
A ball in n dimensions is called a hyperball or n -ball
an (open) n -ball of radius r and center x is the set of all points of distance less than r from x
A closed n -ball of radius r is the set of all points of distance less than or equal to r away from x
every ball is bounded by a hypersphere
The n -dimensional volume of a Euclidean ball of radius r
the double factorial (2 k + 1)!! is defined for odd integers
The surface area of an n-ball
The open (metric) ball of radius r centered at a point p in M
The closed (metric) ball, sometimes denoted
A unit ball (open or closed) is a ball of radius 1
a ball in real coordinate space under the Chebyshev distance is a hypercube

annotations.label: Closed ball in infinite-dim space is not compact

anchor.snippet: a closed ball in any infinite-dimensional normed vector space is never compact

annotations.label: Balls in normed spaces are convex

anchor.snippet: a ball in a normed vector space will always be convex
A subset of a metric space is bounded if it is contained in some ball
A set is totally bounded if, given any positive radius
The open balls of a metric space can serve as a base

annotations.label: Norm from centrally symmetric convex set

anchor.snippet: given any centrally symmetric , bounded , open , and convex subset X of R n , one can define a norm
An (open or closed) n -dimensional topological ball of X is any subset of X which is homeomorphic

annotations.label: Open topological n-ball homeomorphic to R^n

anchor.snippet: Any open topological n -ball is homeomorphic to the Cartesian space R n

annotations.label: Closed topological n-ball homeomorphic to n-cube

anchor.snippet: Any closed topological n -ball is homeomorphic to the closed n -cube

annotations.label: n-ball homeomorphic to m-ball iff n=m

anchor.snippet: An n -ball is homeomorphic to an m -ball if and only if n = m

annotations.label: Topological balls need not be smooth

anchor.snippet: A topological n -ball need not be smooth
